clusters.title: Police raid apartment owned by Annastacia Palaszczuk's partner
clusters.neutral_headline: Police raid apartment owned by Annastacia Palaszczuk's partner
bullet_summary: Police said they are collecting the evidence as a part of an ongoing investigation

items.title: Police raid apartment owned by Annastacia Palaszczuk's partner
items.standfirst: Police have raided a Gold Coast apartment owned by the partner of former Queensland Premier Annastacia Palaszczuk.
items.content: Police have raided a Gold Coast apartment owned by the partner of former Queensland Premier Annastacia Palaszczuk.

items.title: Police search unit owned by partner of ex-premier Annastacia Palaszczuk
items.standfirst: Police said they are collecting the evidence as a part of an ongoing investigation.
items.content: Queensland Police have searched a luxury beachside unit owned by prominent surgeon Dr Reza Adib, the partner of former premier Annastacia Palaszczuk.Officers were seen coming and going from the beachside unit at Burleigh Heads this afternoon and leaving with bags of evidence.Police said they are collecting the evidence as a part of an ongoing investigation.READ MORE: Trump obsession reaches new heights No charges have been laid.Authorities stressed Palaszczuk was not involved and may not even have any knowledge of the situation.The former premier spoke highly of her partner as early as this week, telling A Current Affair's Ally Langdon Adib has been a huge support for her throughout her political career.The pair have been together since 2021.The search comes as Palaszczuk attends an event at Robina Library tonight to promote her new book, The Politics of Being Me.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped the Sky Blues into Origin shape
items.standfirst: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.
items.content: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.
